     The Numbers Business Report

Man on a Ledge (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $18,620,000Details
International Box Office $31,001,440
Worldwide Box Office $49,621,440
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,103,987
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,994,797
Total $8,098,784
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Nick Cassidy makes a desperate and life-threatening move to prove his innocence after he is framed for the theft of a rare, prized diamond. Recently escaped from prison and with nowhere else to go, Nick climbs onto the ledge of a towering skyscraper, inviting the eyes of New York City to anxiously watch as one wrong step could mean plunging to his death. But as one police negotiator soon learns, Nick’s daredevil stunt, captivating the eyes of the public and media, masks a dangerous ploy to reveal the truth about his tarnished name. With the help of his brother and with time running out, Nick’s intricate plan must work perfectly, but when you’re on the 25th floor ledge of a building, going down takes on an entirely more hair-raising meaning.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,001,932 (43.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.33 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$42,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,998 opening theaters/2,998 max. theaters, 2.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $27,976,784

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 27th, 2012 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: February 3rd, 2012 (Wide) (Brazil)
March 16th, 2012 (Wide) (Uruguay)
March 22nd, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
April 5th, 2012 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
April 6th, 2012 (Wide) (Kenya)
Video Release: May 29th, 2012 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2168, 4/20/2011)
Running Time: 102 minutes
Geographical setting: New York, New York City
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, False Identity, Framed, Heist, News, NYPD, Police Detective, Revenge, Suicide, Wrongfully Convicted
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Summit Entertainment, di Bonaventura Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Nick Cassidy makes a desperate and life-threatening move to prove his innocence after he is framed for the theft of a rare, prized diamond. Recently escaped from prison and with nowhere else to go, Nick climbs onto the ledge of a towering skyscraper, inviting the eyes of New York City to anxiously watch as one wrong step could mean plunging to his death. But as one police negotiator soon learns, Nick’s daredevil stunt, captivating the eyes of the public and media, masks a dangerous ploy to reveal the truth about his tarnished name. With the help of his brother and with time running out, Nick’s intricate plan must work perfectly, but when you’re on the 25th floor ledge of a building, going down takes on an entirely more hair-raising meaning.
